description: Apollo 16 Flight Journal - Day 5, Part 3: "I be a sorry bird". LM and CM Problems, Revs 12 and 13

tokens: We will maintain separate transcripts for CM and LM during this frontside pass on Rev 12.
The CM transcript with Ken Mattingly in Casper and Henry Hartsfield as CapCom comes first.
Audio for some of the CM communication is not available to the Journal at this time.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Loud and clear, and let me clean up some things, Hank, and I'll be with you in just a minute.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, look up over my - our right side and look at that antenna, the - the steerable, and see how it - it's moving.
I'm going to move it in pitch, then in yaw.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay: I got it, and it's moving in - It looks like a combination now.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Ken, your T2 time is good.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: I'm trying to get things all straightened up.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: There is a little bit of noise in the background, Ken, but I'm reading you okay.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: I understand that my T2 time is good.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: It looks like my maneuver is going to get me there about - right now.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Yeah, I got out of here roll, 15; pitch, 296; 000.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I was looking at your Noun 89 there and comparing it with the value in the PAD there.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: It's surprising, Hank, how great things are just looking through the telescope and I can't spend much time over here with the naked eye [garble].
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: The things I'm looking at now are really not that far from the terminator.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Casper, you're coming on ten seconds to T1 .
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And we're back in the running.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I told you wrong on that, Ken.
It's a little while to go yet.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay, Casper, you're coming up on T2 now in about ten seconds.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Are you ready for rendezvous radar VHF range check?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: If you'll give us Accept, we'll up-link your state vector and target load.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We'd like to go on and get the Circ PAD up to you.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Give me about 30 seconds, Hank, and I'll give you a call.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: The rest of the PAD is NA.
Two jets, 16 seconds; quads Bravo and Delta.
End of PAD, and the computer is yours.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: I'll read it back to you in just a second.
Two jets, 16 seconds; quads Bravo and Delta.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Good readback, Ken, and I'll give you the abort PADs after you do your P52.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Yeah, let's get to work on these first.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Hank, it looks like we made a little discrepancy here in the way it is now.
It shows we crossed out the Verb 48 at 97:15 to get us a 10101 because we were all ready there, I thought.
And that doesn't look like the case.
I guess we're gonna catch it up here after the burn.
It is probably okay in its present form?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We went to 10102 back at 96:34, and we crossed out the one at 97:15, and we figured that same DAP, and it would hold you through the burn, and then we'll go to the new DAP after the burn.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: The reason is being - is because we do have 509 in there.
We can't do another DAP with it running.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Just want to make sure with all the changes around in this 509 that we hadn't overlooked one.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Houston, what do you say we pass the GDC Align for the COAS calibration maneuver and get back on the time line?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Casper, while you're maneuvering there, would you want to copy the Abort PAD?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Echo, 098:47 all zips; Foxtrot, plus 0102.3, all zips, minus 0050.0; Golf, 099:35 all zips; Hotel, 101:22:15.00; India, 098:35:04.68; Juliet, 101:22:15.00; Kilo 103:21 all zips; Lima, 098:59:29.03; Mike, 105:19:45.00; November, 100:42:42.86.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: That was a super reading, Hank.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And, Casper, for your information, PIPAs and AGS look good.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: I take it we've deleted the rendezvous radar check.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Casper, a preliminary look at your tracking data looks real good.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We have a Go for circ.
And, as a little reminder there, don't forget to terminate the EMP after the burn.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay; the EMP will be terminated after the gimbal drive check.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We're about two minutes from LOS.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Casper's communications restart here after LOS, now starting Orion's communications from AOS.
The recording begins with an announcement from the Public Affairs officer.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: This is Apollo Control; 96 hours, 15 minutes.
About 50 seconds now away from Acquisition Of Signal with Orion and Casper coming around from the backside of the Moon.
Standing by for confirmation from the network controller that the tracking station does have data and downlink from the two spacecraft.

There'll be some trouble shooting around this rev on the steerable antenna on Orion, and a further checkout of the discrepancy in regulator pressures on the Lunar Module Reaction Control System thrusters.
We have AOS confirmation from [the] Network controller.
Let's come up on the air-ground circuit to monitor the first words.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Five by, Jim, and we're sailing free.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It was a little rushed, but we got it done.
The only thing that's bad is that I got a pack full of orange juice.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, we copy, and we'd like you to go through another procedure here to get the S-Band locked up if you're - ready to make - take a few notes?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Tell him to stan - stand by one.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, we're on hot mike still, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: He told us to go ahead.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We wanted to - you to put the steerable at Pitch, minus 75, and Yaw at minus 12.
In other words, the stowed position.
Go Track Mode, Slew, wait 30 seconds, and then go Pitch of plus 63, Yaw of minus 32, and antenna S-Band to Slew and proceed with normal acquisition.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, look up over my - our right side and look at that antenna, the - the steerable, and see how it - it's moving.
I'm going to move it in pitch, then in yaw.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: ...find out what your RCS configuration is at the present time.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: System A and B are Open, Crossfeed is Closed, and the Ascent Feed's been terminated.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Roger; we'd like you to use System A just as long as possible.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, System A is - System A is - is now Open, Crossfeed is Open, and System B is Closed.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And, Orion, have you loaded the AGS abort constants yet?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We don't have the AGS up yet, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Roger; because when you do load those constants, we want you to load nominal values from the data card.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, we're going ahead with the DPS throttle check, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, and, if this S-band procedure doesn't work for us, we're going to ask you to maneuver to an FDAI attitude, where the - the yaw angle will not have to change and we'll see if we can lock up in that attitude.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: What you got to do, Charlie - You are all wet.
Here, get the thing back away from you.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Houston, how do you read on the steerable?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Houston, back on the aft omni.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We'd like you to proceed now with this attitude maneuver.
I'll give you FDAI angles and the steerable angles.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, the FDAI is Roll, 000; Pitch, 053; and Yaw, 000; and the steerable angles: Pitch, plus 26; Yaw, minus 12.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, Houston, we're running through the DPS throttle check.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Orion, the DPS throttle check looks good.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, Jim, I have some Noun 20s for you if you're ready to copy.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Roger; go ahead on the Noun 20s.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay; for the Lunar Module, Orion.
Orion, we've got plus 29603, plus 28563, plus 35951.
For the Command Module, Casper, plus 00004...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Aww, there's still excessive noise down here.
Give me the Command Module numbers again.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, we're - How do you read now?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I can just barely read you, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, we'll read you out - on the Noun 20 for the Command Module.
Four balls 4, plus 10556, plus 00045; that time was at 96:02:20.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We hope you're at about the attitude.
We'd like you to go to an acquisition here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, we're in attitude and we're going to give you the steerable.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: How do you read me on the Forward omni?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Charlie, I just barely read you on the Forward omni.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We just barely hear you, and I have a landing site TCA for you if you're ready to copy.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Roger; can we now do the DPS pressurization checkout?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Roger; we're ready for DPS press.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Can you - Go ahead with the - with the TCA, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Descent Prop Isol Valve is going to Fire.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We are in Downvoice Backup, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And the Ambient Press is 410, the Supercrit Press is 1170.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tell you one thing Charlie's not going to get up here, and that's scurvy.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Orion, the DPS pressurization checkout looks good.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Can you give us our TCA landing site?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'm going to do the landing radar checkout now.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Transponders to Hi Multiple; Select, Landing Radar; H/H-dot; Landing Antenna to Auto.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Radar Test to Landing; power signal light, out.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, Jim, Houston on the steerable, I mean - listen at me - Orion on the steerable.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, we read you much better.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, it worked that time, Jim; we got a 4.2 signal strength, and the steerable is working, I'm in Track Mode, Auto.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I have some words for you on the RCS.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, let's go normal configuration on your RCS and then we want you to transfer three percent more out of System A as we see the pressure going up on A.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And the caution, of course - not more than 180 on the APS.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, the landing radar H-dot is only reading minus 17 right now.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Orion, will you give us Hi bit rate, please?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You got Hi bit rate; Biomed is Left.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Jim, could we try a pitch maneuver back to the landing site viewing attitude, so we'll see if this thing tracks?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We want to get our uplinks in and then you can try that maneuver.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, Orion; let's go P00 and Data and we'll send you an uplink.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, you've got P00 and Data.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Houston, the landing radar test is not working properly.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Well, it's not reading the right numbers in altitude rate and it's not reading the right numbers in Verb 63.
[Pause.] The Altitude Transmitter's 3.2, the Velocity Transmitter - Velocity Transmitter's 3.7.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And, Jim, the AGS is loaded with the data card - go ahead.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, we want you to select Normal Voice.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, you have Normal Voice, AGS is loaded with the - with the data card numbers.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Are you also showing bad data on the tapemeter for the landing radar?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: The H - altitude is reading right at 8000, but the velocity was only reading 15.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Can I run it while P27 is in progress?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Hold off on that landing radar check until after the - the uplink.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: [Pause.] We'll go off and pull the circuit breakers.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Jim, we'd like to start a pitch - back down so we could see the landing site.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I have the Abort PADs whenever you're all ready.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: 098:47 all zeros, plus 0102.3, plus all zeros, minus 0050.0; 0138.0, plus 0011.0, 0113.9; 0:35; all zeros, 273; 5927.0; plus 0102.6, plus all zeros, minus 0049.4; 099:35 all zeros; 101:22:15.00.
Throttle profile 10 percent for 26 seconds; full throttle for remainder; LM weight, 36673.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: That was a little bit too fast, but I think I got it all.
LM - that throttle profile is 10 percent for 26 seconds, then full throttle; LM weight, 36673.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Let me just confirm that Noun 86 Delta-VXis plus 0102.6, and we're finished with your computer.
Okay, and we need an E-Mod dump for...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, and I'm going into the landing radar check again.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And, Charlie, I'm ready on the - the PDI PAD.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, I was wrong on that.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, are you ready for PDI?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Go ahead with the PDI PAD.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Could we start a pitch attitude down to see the landing site?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Are you done with our E-Mod, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We're finished with the E-Mod dump, but we'd just as soon get all these PADs up, and we're not concerned about the landing site - down here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I - I'm - I'm down through Kilo and I'll read back starting at India.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, that's a good readback, and I have T2 and T3 for you.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Orion, will you verify Auto on the steerable?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, and reading back starting with Lima, 098:59:29.03; 105:19:45.00; November, 100:42:42.86.
Go ahead with the next one.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, we're standing by for the - the landing radar checkout, John; and, of course, Charlie, you got that T2 at PDI at 24 plus 25, and I have an AGS K-factor for you.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, go ahead with the AGS.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It's reading all right in H-dot, but it's changing data in - in the next two registers.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Roger; we're looking at it down here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And the tapemeters is now - and the tapemeter is now reading 480 opening, and the altitude meter would - The first time I did it, it read 8000, and it's now reading zero.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Let's go Lo bit rate, and we're losing the steerable.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Jim, I don't think it's tracking in yaw.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Orion, we'll get back to you on the landing radar.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...Houston, just a reminder on - to load 405 and 406 to plus zero.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And we're ready for Hi bit rate.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, I'm going to terminate the landing radar test, if that's okay with y'all.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That's negative; Houston wants them to stay locked on right now.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I have the Circ PAD if you're ready to copy.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, ignition at 097:40:17.16; Noun 81s, plus 0068.1, minus all zeros, minus 0058.0.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, we'll go ahead and go the - do the IMU fine align right now, if that's okay with you, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, Charlie, will you - In 404, will you put minus 12345?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, Houston, when we do this attitude maneuver for the P52, we're going to lose high gain.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I think we're all prepared for it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, you can go ahead and maneuver, John...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: and we want you to use the RCS system A.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, Houston, we have you on the aft omni.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The torquing angles: minus 0.060, plus 0.139, minus 0.018.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, may - perhaps we can read the torquing angles, if you want to give them again.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Star angle difference was minus four balls five.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, we copied your Noun 05, but we did not get the torquing angles.
I hope y'all have written them down.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We'd like to get Hi Bit Rate again and some good voice.
We'd like you to go back to that attitude which we gave you of pitch of 053 and set in the steerable angles of Pitch plus 26 and Yaw minus 12.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And just delay the landing radar test until we get some good data.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I guess on that landing radar - with the circuit breaker in - probably working off the ground - maybe not.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Orion, let's go back to Lo bit rate until we get the steerable.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, Jim, we have you now on the steerable.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Oh, I read you loud and clear.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Our torquing angles were minus 0.060, plus 0.139, minus 0.018.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: On torquing angles, minus 0.060, plus 0.139, and minus 0 - 0.018 at 96:58:40.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And you can go Normal voice.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And the AGS checkout has gone well.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The only thing we haven't done is Rendezvous Radar checkout and we'll get to that soon as Ken gets through with his burn.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Roger; we're recommending that Rendezvous Radar checkout on the back side, and Landing Radar checkout is the one we want to go through now.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, it's up and left like it's supposed to be.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, John, when you get to the Noun 66...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...67 values, we want you to read us the tapemeter values of H and H-dot.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, minus 495, plus 1860, plus 1331, right on, and the tapemeter is up and left and it's reading 8000 at 480.
I think it was locked on the ground or something when we came over that low pass, due to our communications angle.
That may be wrong, but that's - you know - it was sure acting funny.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, it's looking good to us now.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, we've got 3.2 on the Altitude Transmitter and 345 on the Velocity Transmitter; make that 355.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Hey, Jim, on those drink bags, I tell you, it's pretty hard to see things when you've got a helmet full of orange juice in zero gravity, it's something with that orange juice.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, you've got to drink fast.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: When do we get the 210 up, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, acquisition on your next rev.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Hey, Jim, we had to turn on our window heaters for about ten minutes per side to clear up the windows - right aft - right before undocking.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We've been using the - we've been using the LCG pump to keep cool in here, and it's really neat.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We've been needing something to keep cool, I'll tell you.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Jim, your uplink voice is just beautiful in every antenna we got.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, that's a good data point.
Unfortunately, the downlink is very, very noisy.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: On the check - the comm checks we did at 55 hours, of course, it was closer, but it was real good then, I thought.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, we understand it's just a completely different situation, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: But your voice is just crystal clear right now.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Orion, this is Houston with some trajectory information for you.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It looks like you'll be coming in about 10,000 feet [3,000 metres] high at PDI, John, which will be about 3 to 4 seconds of hover time, and you'll be 17,000 feet [5,000 metres]...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, does that mean that we're going to be - at pitchover, we'll be steering from south to north?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, so at pitchover, we'll be - you'll be targeting us right into the target, but we'll be steering from south to north.
Is that based on Ken's tracking?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: No, that's not - Negative on that one.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: But you'll probably be coming straight in by the time you get down to pitchover.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: How did landmark tracking turn out ?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, the landmark tracking looked very good, John.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Orion, will you give us your ED Bat read-out, please?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Jim, is Guidance going to have any gyro drift for us ?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And it looks like your - the attitude for PDI is very close to the one that we'd like for the s - steerable, so we'll try that when you come around at AOS.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It - that radar was - has drifted up into the field of view, but it's no sweat just moving it down in slew.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And one other thing that - when we put those state vectors in there, I guess we didn't have any - the LM vector in there and my Computer Activity light stayed on all the time.
I finally figured out what it was and did a Verb 66 and got rid of it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I think - I think that's what it was.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Everybody's nodding their head down here - affirmative.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Houston, 16 - what appears to be the problem with System A.
Is it a reg[ulator] problem or what?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yes, that's affirmative, Charlie, a reg problem.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, will we have a - If we use up fuel - just System A for descent, is what you want us to do.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We'll give you an RCS configuration for PDI when y'all come around the corner.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And I'd like somebody to think about this high APS pressure we have during the lunar stay.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, we're looking at that one too, John.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, we are noticing an increase in the RCS pressure there, but we have enough ullage volume now to get the - all the propellant out.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yes, If we were on hot mike...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: ...ever noticed any change in your yaw meter?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I think that if we're on hot mike when we're talking to each other, I want to apologize right now.
Probably not, if the comm was as bad as you said it was.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It was good enough for us to understand you.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: If you see that reg pressure creeping up, you could do a small maneuver, which would help the situation.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We'll do a Verb 49 for the AGS cal attitude, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: [Pause.] And, Orion, we're coming up on about two minutes to LOS.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: For your information the burst disk pressure is 215 to 220 - the RCS.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Jim, is it both systems you see climbing?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Hey, Jim, I saw the landing site as we passed over it.
We're not going to have any trouble recognizing it from the rays.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And both spacecraft have passed behind the Moon during the end of the 12th lunar orbit.
During this past rather interesting frontside pass, several nagging problems have cropped up or have been carried over from the preceding revolution.
One concern is the Landing Radar which, in its self-test mode, gave some spurious readouts, the readouts never agreeing with what the test should be.
Later on in a repetition of the Landing Radar self-test, the numbers on the onboard display came out as they should.

John Young speculated that perhaps because of the low altitude at the time of the first attempt at the Landing Radar self-test, they were getting some ground reflections from the lunar surface which caused the self-test to be invalid.
The self-test of the Rendezvous Radar has been postponed until the upcoming backside pass prior to acquisition on Rev 13.
The steerable antenna situation is still coming in and out as the steerable antenna appears to be locked in a stowed position.
However, with the spacecraft attitude, oriented such that the antenna faces the Earth, we've had fairly good communications during the better part of this front side pass.

The 210-foot [64-metre] dish at Goldstone will acquire the spacecraft at the start of Rev 13, and even if the steerable antenna is completely inoperative, all high-bit-rate data, communications, voice; everything normally carried by the steerable antenna through the 85-foot [26-metre] dishes will be available on the ground.

[On] the Lunar Module Reaction Control System regulator situation, the crew is still running through some procedures to manage the two systems, A and B, of the Reaction Control System aboard the Lunar Module to balance the regulator pressures for the propellants.
System A appears to creep upward slightly, periodically, and by opening the crossfeed valve and venting some of the propellant into the ascent tanks, it appears that the situation will stabilize.
The Descent Propulsion System throttle check and the Descent Propulsion System pressurization routines were carried out completely normally earlier in Revolution 12.
Ken Mattingly, meanwhile, was given a Go to circularize with the Command Module, Casper.
Ignition time for this burn is 97 hours, 40 minutes, 17 seconds.
It will be a Service Propulsion System burn, [of] 99.6 feet per second, which will circularize at about 51.8 by 68.2 [nautical miles, 95.9 by 126.3 km].
This is slightly elliptical but because of the perturbations of lunar orbit, the orbit of the Command Module should be almost circular at the time of rendezvous.
Acquisition: 43 minutes, 23 seconds from now, as both spacecraft come around the front side.
None of the problems mentioned with the RC - Reaction Control System regulators or the steerable antenna preclude the lunar landing.
Even if the S-band - steerable S-band antenna is completely inoperative, we're still Go for landing using the Omni antennas through the Goldstone 210-foot dish.
42 minutes, 36 seconds away from acquisition on the PDI - or landing orbit.
At 97:28 Ground Elapsed Time, this is Apollo Control.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: For this period until AOS, both Orion's and Casper's transcripts are presented together.
transcript.speaker: Commentary
